private_opportunities.title: McDonald's Plans to Open 8,000 Restaurants by End of 2027 and ...
private_opportunities.snippet:
# McDonald’s Plans to Open 8,000 Restaurants by End of 2027 and Shake Up Menu

Published

Jan 20, 2026 at 11:12 AM EST

updated

Jan 21, 2026 at 09:37 AM EST

Amanda Greenwood

Associate News Editor

McDonalds is planning to complete its global expansion of more than 8,000 new restaurants across 50,000 locations by 2027, and this year, also plans to change its pricing structure and menu offerings.

## What Changes Is McDonald’s Planning?

### Global Expansion

McDonald’s plans to reach 50,000 locations worldwide by the end of 2027, including the opening of more than 8,000 new restaurants (about 900 in the U.S.), an initiative that began in 2024. The expansion is designed to position the company for value‑focused consumers and to intensify competition in high‑growth international markets. [...] Correction 1/21/26, 9:36 a.m. ET: This article was corrected to state that McDonald's plans to complete its global expansion by the end of 2027 and to clarify its new pricing guidelines.

Google Preferred Source Banner

## Related Podcasts

## Top Stories

Democrats’ Pipe Dream of Turning Texas Blue Takes Step Forward
The 5 Ways Khamenei’s Iran Survival Plan Haunts Trump
The Fight for the Post-Trump Republican Party Starts in Texas
Nicole M. Amor’s Husband Recalls Final Call With Killed US Soldier
US Launches Military Action In Ecuador: What To Know
How Food Is Replacing Pills as Medicine at the Top US Hospitals

## Trending [...] ### New Menu Changes

Some popular CosMc's offerings are being introduced to the menu after the experimental, drinks-forward shops shuttered in June 2025. According to McDonald’s, the Toasted Vanilla Frappé, Strawberry Watermelon Refresher, Sprite Lunar Splash, Popping Tropic Refresher, and a Creaming Vanilla Cold Brew will now be available at 500 McDonald's locations.

New sandwich options and chicken items are likely to be part of future menu updates, as customer demand for chicken products now matches that for beef items, Mashed reported.

The restaurant said its “Better Burger Initiative,” which focuses on improving burger preparation, using fresher ingredients, and implementing stricter food-handling standards, will also revolutionize the quality of its burgers.

private_opportunities.title: McDonald's CEO confirms 8,000 new stores opening in major ...
private_opportunities.snippet:
More from The Sun

# McDonald’s CEO confirms 8,000 new stores opening in major expansion push

Plus, read up on how the chain grew into the global fast food superpower as we know it today

McDonald’s CEO confirms 8,000 new stores opening in major expansion push

THE top boss of McDonald’s has announced that the fast food giant has some major expansion plans in the works, on a mission to grow its global presence.

The chain’s CEO, Chris Kempczinski, shared McDonald’s plan to roll out over 8,000 new restaurants by the end of 2027.

### Sign up for the Money newsletter

### Thank you!

Please provide a valid email.

Your info will be used in accordance with our Privacy Policy

You'll now receive top stories, breaking news, and more, straight to your email.

NINTCHDBPICT001036321476 [...] The goal to have 50,000 McDonald’s locations by 2027’s end marks the fastest period of growth in the chain’s history.

“As I’ve said before, there has never been a better time to be part of McDonald’s,” Kempczinski said when the chain first announced the expansion plans in 2023.

“We have a clear trajectory for future growth as we continue to build on the brand strength, global footprint, and digital ecosystem that have resulted in unparalleled competitive advantages and cemented McDonald’s as one of the world’s leading consumer-facing brands.”

## TASTY SUCCESS

McDonald’s has seen great success since it was founded over eight decades ago.

The first location opened back in 1940, but it was not McDonald’s as we know it now. [...] NINTCHDBPICT001036321476

McDonald’s is on a mission to expand globally as it races to regain its title of largest fast food chain in the worldCredit: Alamy

BIZ-MCDONALDS-CEO-CHICAGO-TB

McDonald’s, one of the most iconic fast food chains, has lost its crown as the largest quick service restaurant in the world to the Chinese company Mixue Bingcheng as of late 2024.

Despite being dethroned, the Golden Arches still operates a huge global network of over 41,800 spots across more than 100 countries, holding steady as a dominant force in the industry, known for its burgers, crispy fries, and global recognition. 

At the chain’s third quarter 2025 earnings conference call on Wednesday morning, top boss Kempczinski shared that McDonald’s has even more global reach in its sights.

private_opportunities.title: McDonald's Unveils Major Global Expansion and ...
private_opportunities.snippet:
#### McDonald's Growth Strategy & Loyalty Program Boost: ‍

McDonald's {{ m-tag option="price" ticker="MCD" currency="USD" }} has announced a significant expansion plan to open approximately 10,000 new restaurants globally by 2027. This move marks one of the fastest growth phases in the company's history. Alongside this expansion, McDonald's focuses on doubling its loyalty program sales to $45 billion and increasing its user base to 250 million by 2027. The current loyalty program boasts 150 million users, contributing over $20 billion in annual sales. [...] ‍

‍

#### Introduction of CosMc's Spinoff: ‍

In a strategic move, McDonald's is set to open its first CosMc's spinoff restaurant in Bolingbrook, Illinois, with plans for 10 locations by the end of 2024. This test phase will determine the potential expansion of this small-format chain. CosMc's, named after a McDonaldland mascot, will offer classic items and new snacks, targeting the afternoon market segment with customizable drinks and coffee.

‍

#### Future Prospects & Past Challenges: ‍ [...] Despite the fluctuating U.S. consumer spending, McDonald's has maintained a strong position, which can be attributed to its affordable menu, aggressive promotions, and effective marketing strategies. The company's CEO, Chris Kempczinski, highlights the resilience of the consumer market, though cautious about future predictions.

‍

#### International Expansion Focus: ‍

A significant portion of the new stores, around 7,000, will be opened in international developmental licensed markets, including China, India, Japan, and Brazil. More than half of these stores are planned for China, McDonald's second-largest market, following a recent deal enhancing the company's control over its operations there.

Leadership should prioritize immediate engagement with McDonald's global procurement team to explore collaboration opportunities, leveraging our NOMYU QSR product line to enhance their customer experience and operational efficiency.
